content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

Your task is to determine the **minimum** number of elements that need to be removed from the array AAA such that the bitwise OR of the remaining elements is 2x−12^x - 12x−1 for some integer x (x≥0)x \ (x\geq 0)x (x≥0).

Note that the bitwise OR of an empty array is considered to be 000.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of elements that need to be removed from the array.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

If we don't remove any element then bitwise OR will be 222222 which is not of the form 2x−12^x - 12x−1.
If we remove 666, then bitwise OR will be 161616 which is not of the form 2x−12^x - 12x−1. Similarly, if we remove 161616 then bitwise OR will be 666 which is not of the form 2x−12^x - 12x−1.
If we remove both elements then bitwise OR is 000 which is of the form 2x−12^x - 12x−1 for x=0x = 0x=0.

Test case 222: If we remove A2=17A_2=17A2​=17 and A3=18A_3=18A3​=18, then bitwise OR of remaining elements will be 777 which is of the form 2x−12^x - 12x−1 for x=3x = 3x=3.
It can be shown that we cannot satisfy the condition by removing less than 222 elements.
